title: Fiery Filipino Pork Stew
description: A charred, chewy Mexican-style pork stew infused with Filipino flavors and Whole30-friendly ingredients, featuring ground allspice and shio-cure for a bold kick.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium‑high and add 2 tbsp coconut oil.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until aromatic.
Season pork cubes with the shio cure and ground allspice mixture.
Increase heat to high and sear pork until all sides are charred.
Pour in tomato sauce, fish sauce, calamansi juice, and 2 cups water or broth; stir to combine.
Add bay leaves and black pepper, bring to a boil, then reduce to low and simmer covered for 45 minutes.
Remove the lid and let the stew reduce to a thick consistency, about 15 more minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then serve hot with steamed rice or cauliflower rice.
